hypothesis: Multi-fidelity Bayesian optimization that uses fast MD/GCMC estimates as low-fidelity observations and long-timescale MD simulations as high-fidelity observations will exhibit a stronger dependence on polymer-specific molecular representations (PolyNC, polyBERT) relative to general chemical space representations when the correlation between fidelity levels is low, because polymer-specific embeddings encode backbone connectivity and repeat-unit topology that are predictive of the conformational reorganization occurring between short and long simulation timescales, and this advantage will be quantifiably larger than that observed in single-fidelity Bayesian optimization under equivalent computational budgets.
